agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, payment, promotions, and post-sale refund lifecycle. The domain enforces inventory integrity, pricing consistency, and order immutability after payment.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout bounded context, with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions/Pricing, and Returns/Refunds contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of items a customer intends to purchase. Holding items in a cart does NOT reserve inventory."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-bound hold placed on inventory units when checkout begins, guaranteeing availability for the duration of the checkout window."},{"term":"Checkout Window","definition":"The 15-minute period starting when checkout begins, during which inventory is reserved and the customer must complete payment."},{"term":"Abandoned Checkout","definition":"A checkout session where payment was not confirmed within the 15-minute checkout window, causing all reservations to be released."},{"term":"Active Promotion","definition":"A currently-in-effect price reduction applied to a product at the catalog level. A product may have at most one active promotion at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A customer-supplied code that applies a discount at the order level, distinct from catalog-level promotional pricing."},{"term":"Discount Mechanism","definition":"Any source of price reduction — either an active promotion or a discount code. Only one mechanism may apply per order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmed","definition":"The irreversible state transition after which an order becomes immutable. No modifications are permitted post-confirmation."},{"term":"Listed Product","definition":"A product visible in the catalog, which may or may not have available inventory. Visibility does not imply purchasability."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-day period following delivery during which a customer may request a refund."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Adding items to a cart does not reserve inventory. Inventory is only reserved when the checkout process begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If cart additions reserved inventory, a malicious or idle user could lock out stock indefinitely, corrupting inventory availability for all other customers. Reservation must be tied to the time-bound checkout process.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: After adding an item to cart, the available inventory count for that SKU is unchanged."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"If payment is not confirmed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation, the inventory reservation is released and the checkout session is abandoned.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute timeout is a business policy threshold. A VP could change it to 10 or 20 minutes. The concept of a timeout-based release IS invariant, but the specific duration is policy.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Given a checkout started at T, if no payment is confirmed by T+15min, the reservation is released and the inventory becomes available again."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on a single product would create pricing ambiguity — the system cannot deterministically resolve which price to display or charge. This is a data consistency constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For any product P at any point in time, COUNT(active promotions for P) <= 1."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing. Only one discount mechanism applies per order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing stacking of discount mechanisms would produce incorrect pricing and unintended margin erosion. The system must enforce mutual exclusivity to maintain pricing integrity.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For any order, it is impossible for both a discount code AND a product-level promotion to contribute to the final price calculation simultaneously."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment immutability is a fundamental integrity constraint. Modifications after payment would break financial reconciliation, audit trails, and fulfillment processes. Corrections must go through refund/exchange flows.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any mutation attempt on an order in 'payment_confirmed' or later state is rejected with an error."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, regardless of its catalog listing status.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling inventory that does not exist would result in overselling — a corrupted state that breaks fulfillment commitments. This is a physical constraint: you cannot ship what you do not have.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any attempt to begin checkout for a product with available_inventory == 0 is rejected, even if the product is listed/visible."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days of the delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a business policy that could be extended for VIP customers, holiday seasons, or regulatory changes. The concept of a refund window is stable, but the duration is policy.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A refund request submitted more than 30 days after the recorded delivery date is rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"They have not initiated checkout","then":"The available inventory for those items remains unchanged and other customers can purchase the same items"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with items in their cart","when":"Sufficient inventory exists for all items","then":"Inventory is reserved for those items, reducing available inventory by the requested quantities"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has an active checkout session with reserved inventory","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment confirmation","then":"All inventory reservations for that session are released, available inventory is restored, and the checkout session is marked as abandoned"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer completes payment within the 15-minute window","when":"Payment is confirmed","then":"The reservation converts to a committed allocation, the order becomes immutable, and no further modifications are allowed"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An attempt is made to add a second promotion to the same product","then":"The system rejects the new promotion and returns an error indicating only one active promotion is permitted"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer applies a discount code to an order containing a product with an active promotion","when":"The order total is calculated","then":"Only one discount mechanism is applied — either the discount code OR the promotional price — and the other is excluded"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order has payment confirmed","when":"Any modification is attempted (add item, remove item, change quantity, change address)","then":"The modification is rejected with an error stating the order is immutable after payment"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A product is listed in the catalog with zero available inventory","when":"A customer attempts to add it to checkout","then":"The purchase attempt is rejected with a clear out-of-stock message"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A customer received their order 25 days ago","when":"They submit a refund request","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A customer received their order 31 days ago","when":"They submit a refund request","then":"The refund request is rejected as outside the 30-day refund window"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"Two customers have the same item in their carts and only one unit remains in inventory","when":"Both initiate checkout simultaneously","then":"Only one customer successfully reserves the item; the other receives an insufficient-inventory error"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Inventory drops to zero after cart addition but before checkout initiation","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation fails with an out-of-stock error. The cart may still show the item, but reservation cannot be created."},{"scenario":"Product promotion is activated while a customer has a discount code applied at checkout","expected_behavior":"The system enforces mutual exclusivity at order finalization. The order uses whichever single mechanism was applied; the newly activated promotion does not retroactively stack."},{"scenario":"Customer's checkout timer expires at the exact moment payment is submitted","expected_behavior":"The system must resolve this atomically. If payment confirmation is received before the reservation release transaction commits, payment succeeds. Otherwise, payment fails and funds are not captured (or are immediately reversed)."},{"scenario":"Refund requested on the exact 30th day after delivery","expected_behavior":"The request is accepted — the 30-day window is inclusive of day 30."},{"scenario":"Product inventory reaches zero due to reservation (not purchase) — another customer tries to checkout","expected_behavior":"The second customer is blocked. Reserved inventory is not available for new reservations, even though no sale is confirmed yet."},{"scenario":"A promotion is deactivated while a customer is mid-checkout with that promotional price","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout initiation should be honored for the duration of the checkout window. Deactivating a promotion does not retroactively change in-flight checkouts."},{"scenario":"Customer abandons checkout, reservation releases, and inventory is now zero from other sales","expected_behavior":"The customer's cart items remain but they cannot re-enter checkout for the out-of-stock item."},{"scenario":"Discount code and promotional pricing both present — which one wins?","expected_behavior":"The spec states they cannot be combined but does not specify priority. The system must either: (a) apply whichever is more favorable to the customer, (b) apply whichever was applied first, or (c) force the customer to choose. This requires a business decision."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Inventory reservation with timeout prevents overselling (costly fulfillment failures and customer compensation). Single-discount-mechanism rule protects margins from unintended discount stacking. 30-day refund window bounds financial exposure from returns.","operational":"15-minute checkout timeout prevents inventory deadlocks and ensures stock fluidity. Order immutability post-payment simplifies fulfillment pipeline — no mid-flight changes to pick/pack/ship. Single-promotion constraint simplifies pricing engine and prevents catalog pricing errors.","user":"Customers see accurate inventory availability. Clear refund window sets expectations. However, no cart-level reservation means customers may lose items between cart and checkout, which could frustrate users during high-demand periods.","risk":"Race conditions at checkout for low-inventory items could cause poor UX if not handled gracefully. The 15-minute timeout must be enforced reliably — a failure to release reservations would silently lock inventory. Discount mechanism mutual exclusivity requires clear UX to avoid customer confusion at checkout.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Cannot hold inventory by carting; may lose items to other buyers. Clear refund window. Cannot modify orders post-payment — must use returns flow."},{"group":"Merchandising/Marketing Team","impact":"Constrained to one promotion per product. Must coordinate promotion scheduling carefully. Discount codes and promotions are mutually exclusive, limiting campaign flexibility."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability simplifies reconciliation. Single discount mechanism per order simplifies revenue recognition. 30-day refund window bounds return accrual liability."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"No mid-flight order changes after payment. Inventory reservations are time-bound and predictable. Overselling risk is minimized."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle complaints about lost cart items during high demand. Must enforce 30-day refund policy. Cannot modify confirmed orders — must guide customers through refund/reorder flow."}]},"confidence":{"level":75,"reasoning":"The specification is explicit and internally consistent. All seven rules are clearly stated with no contradictions. Confidence is not higher because one business decision remains unspecified (discount priority resolution) and the rules cover the happy path well but leave some operational details implicit (e.g., timezone for 30-day window, exact semantics of reservation atomicity).","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory is not reserved at cart time — only at checkout","15-minute reservation timeout and release","One active promotion per product","Order immutability after payment confirmation","Zero-inventory products cannot be purchased","Discount code and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which discount mechanism takes priority when both are present — customer choice, first-applied, or best-for-customer? The spec says they cannot combine but does not specify the resolution strategy.","Whether the 30-day refund window is calendar days or business days, and whether day 30 is inclusive","Whether the 15-minute timeout is hard (server-enforced) or soft (client-side with server validation)","Price locking semantics — whether the price at checkout initiation is honored if promotions change mid-session"],"assumptions":["The 30-day refund window is measured in calendar days and is inclusive of day 30","Inventory reservation is server-side and atomically enforced","The 15-minute timeout is measured from the moment the checkout session is created on the server","Order immutability means no field on the order aggregate can be changed — not just line items but also shipping address, etc."]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, payment, promotions, and post-sale refund lifecycle. The domain enforces inventory integrity, pricing consistency, and order immutability after payment.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout bounded context, with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions/Pricing, and Returns/Refunds contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of items a customer

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this e-commerce domain scenario carefully.

The rules are clear and well-defined. Let me classify each business rule and determine if there are any escalations needed.

Looking at the rules:
1. Cart doesn't reserve inventory - reservation at checkout
2. 15-minute checkout timeout releases reservation
3. Max one active promotion per product
4. Discount codes and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive
5. Orders immutable after payment confirmation
6. Zero inventory = can't purchase even if listed
7. 30-day refund window from delivery

These are all clearly stated. No contradictions, no significant ambiguity, no missing context. The spec is quite clear.


I'm noticing one potential gap: when discount codes and promotional pricing can't combine, there's no explicit priority rule for which takes precedence. There's also the edge case of inventory dropping to zero mid-checkout, though the reservation mechanism should handle that. Overall though, the specification is solid enough that I don't think escalation is necessary—I'd put my confidence around 72-75%.
